extraction.one_line_summary: BALLERINI Davide won the Krakow sprint from position behind ACKERMANN Pascal's early launch, coming through late despite no prior WorldTour victories.
tactical_patterns: ACKERMANN Pascal launched sprint first from Stuyven leadout but lacked finishing speed despite strong early jump position.
BALLERINI Davide deliberately sat behind ACKERMANN's early acceleration rather than contesting initial sprint positions, timing finish surge better.
Trek-Segafredo worked hardest to control break despite not having designated sprinter, revealing STUYVEN's ability to sprint from leadout role.
CAVENDISH Mark positioned well under 1km but mechanical pedal unclip eliminated him, showing equipment failure risk in final positioning chaos.

rider_signals.BALLERINI Davide: positive: First WorldTour win reveals he finishes stronger when drafting early launchers rather than leading sprint himself.
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: Opened sprint too early and faded despite Giro points jersey form, suggesting leadout timing issues cost him.
rider_signals.STUYVEN Jasper: neutral: Led out sprint despite being classics rider not pure sprinter, Trek's lack of designated fast finisher exposed.
rider_signals.BAUHAUS Phil: neutral: Designated Bahrain-McLaren sprinter but couldn't match top-end speed of ACKERMANN or BALLERINI in finale.
rider_signals.DAINESE Alberto: positive: Third place suggests emerging sprint talent when positioned correctly behind main contenders.
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: Well-positioned under 1km but pedal mechanical proves even experienced positioning negated by equipment failure.
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: Won GC by 1:52 over FUGLSANG with 50km Stage 4 attack, fourth straight stage race win.
rider_signals.VAN EMDEN Jos: neutral: Joined late breakaway that held 22 seconds at 12km remaining, showing Azure willingness to commit multiple riders.
